description:
You are a build-and-release engineer helping an artifact-management team curate a plain-text inventory
  of binaries stored in several internal repositories.  
  All work must be done inside the directory
  /home/user/repos-inventory (already created for you).

  1. Inside that directory there is an input file named
     /home/user/repos-inventory/artifacts.tsv  
     The file is TAB-separated and **has exactly five columns, in this order**:

        1. repo_id          (a short identifier such as “core”, “ml”, …)  
        2. artifact_name    (groupId:artifactId form, may itself contain a colon)  
        3. version          (semantic version string)  
        4. size_kb          (integer, size of the binary in kibibytes)  
        5. sha256           (a 64-character hexadecimal digest)

     There is one header line followed by 10 data lines.

  2. Your job is to create two derivative files **using only standard
     text-processing utilities (e.g. cut, paste, sort, uniq, awk,
     grep, sed, head, tail, etc.)**.  The entire task can be completed
     with roughly 5–10 shell commands.

     a. /home/user/repos-inventory/curated_artifacts.txt  
        • Each output line must be in the form  
          repo_id:artifact_name@version  
        • Preserve the original order of the records as they appear in
          artifacts.tsv (i.e. do not sort).  
        • The file must contain *no header*, exactly 10 non-empty lines,
          each terminated by a single newline (Unix line ending).

     b. /home/user/repos-inventory/big_artifacts.log  
        • Keep only those records whose size_kb is strictly greater than
          10240.  
        • For every such large artifact output a line formatted as  
          repo_id|artifact_name|size_kb  
        • Lines must be ordered by appearance in the source file
          (first match first).  
        • No header line; each data line must end with a newline.

  3. When you are finished, display the contents of both newly-created
     files on the terminal so that a reviewer can see the results.

  4. The automatic grader will check:

        • That curated_artifacts.txt and big_artifacts.log exist at the
          exact paths given above.  
        • That their line counts and line formats are correct.  
        • That the content matches what is expected from the original
          artifacts.tsv data set.

  Only the two new files need to be created or modified; do not edit the
  original artifacts.tsv.  No extra files should be produced.
truth:
The file /home/user/repos-inventory/artifacts.tsv **already exists** and has the
  following exact content (TAB characters separate the columns; each
  line ends with a single LF, no trailing spaces):

utils<TAB>org.tools:zipper<TAB>0.9.8<TAB>5120<TAB>d7a4b5a8934dd94dcd4b4a6e4b3e2c8fd2e9adf3edd5c6b1b3a9e5d8f6a7b2d3

ml<TAB>ai.deep:onnx<TAB>1.8.1<TAB>9200<TAB>aabbccddeeff00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff00112233445566778899
java<TAB>org.apache:log4j<TAB>2.14.1<TAB>800<TAB>99887766554433221100ffeeddccbbaa99887766554433221100ffeeddccbbaa
